response.justification: Candidate is a skilled full-stack developer with strong web/backend experience, but the role requires 8 years of C++ programming and 5 years building large-scale infrastructure/distributed systems, which are not demonstrated. C++ exposure is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and an undergraduate thesis. Primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, and there is no evidence of cloud compute platform leadership or production-level cloud architecture work at the scale this role demands.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with mentoring, workshops, and community teaching experience
Hands-on experience with high-throughput micro-services (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) and TDD/CI/CD practices
Founder of an open-source geo-data platform deployable on Kubernetes, showing ownership of ambiguous projects
concerns: Falls far short of the 8 years of C++ requirement — only brief C/C++ work at National Instruments
No demonstrated experience leading or architecting an internal cloud compute platform
Lacks formal technical-leadership title and explicit large-scale distributed-systems / cloud architecture track record

key_factors.factor: No cloud compute platform or large-scale distributed-systems leadership
Breadth of engineering experience and mentoring
